name: Castro Alto de la Cruz Loop
name_original: Castro Alto de la Cruz (1.473 m) – circular desde Arévalo de la Sierra
description: Hike to the ancient Castro Alto de la Cruz hillfort on this moderate 5.4-mile (8.6 km) circular route from Arévalo de la Sierra.
region_intro: Hiking around Arévalo De La Sierra offers diverse terrain at the foot of the Sierra de Alba in Soria, Spain. The region is characterized by the extensive Acebal de Garagüeta, the largest holly forest in the Iberian Peninsula, alongside pine forests and river valleys. Hikers can explore varied landscapes, from dense woodlands to ancient Celtiberian settlements and natural pools formed by the Río Rulaque. This area provides a range of hiking opportunities across different elevations and environments. Best hiking trails around Arévalo De La Sierra The most popular hiking route is Senda del Acebal Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 36 minutes to complete. This easy route leads through the unique holly forest landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castro Alto de la Cruz Loop, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.6 km) path. This route explores the area around an ancient Celtiberian settlement, offering historical context alongside natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Ermita Santos nuevos y Alto de Tabanera – circular desde San Andrés de Soria, a 14.0 miles (22.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 41 minutes. Hiking around Arévalo De La Sierra is defined by dense holly forests, pine woodlands, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Arévalo De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Arévalo De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Arévalo de la Sierra?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from the dense, labyrinthine woodlands of the &lt;strong&gt;Acebal de Garagüeta&lt;/strong&gt;, the largest holly forest in the Iberian Peninsula, to varied landscapes at the foot of the Sierra de Alba. You'll also find extensive pine forests and river valleys, providing a mix of environments for different hiking experiences.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, there are several easy options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063912767/senda-del-acebal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Acebal Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.5 km route through the unique holly forest. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garagüeta Holly Forest – La Turruca Stone Hut loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.76 km and also rated easy.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Arévalo de la Sierra?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063933801/castro-alto-de-la-cruz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Alto de la Cruz Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.6 km path that explores an ancient Celtiberian settlement. For a longer challenge, consider the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062391615/ermita-santos-nuevos-y-alto-de-tabanera-circular-desde-san-andres-de-soria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita Santos nuevos y Alto de Tabanera – circular desde San Andrés de Soria&lt;/a&gt;, a 22.5 km trail.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I explore while hiking in the area?
A: Beyond the stunning &lt;strong&gt;Acebal de Garagüeta Nature Reserve&lt;/strong&gt; (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;highlight&lt;/a&gt;), you can discover ancient sites like &lt;strong&gt;Castro de El Castellar&lt;/strong&gt;, a Celtiberian settlement. The area also features natural pools formed by the Río Rulaque, such as "El Chorrón," and extensive pine forests. You might even find traces of wildlife like deer, wild boar, and foxes.

Q: Are there any trails that offer historical insights?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063933801/castro-alto-de-la-cruz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Alto de la Cruz Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the area of an ancient Celtiberian settlement, offering a blend of hiking and historical exploration. You can walk through what was once a fortified enclosure, where archaeological remains have been found.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Arévalo de la Sierra?
A: The region offers pleasant hiking conditions across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, with mild temperatures and vibrant foliage. Summer can be warm, but the shade of the holly and pine forests, along with natural pools like "El Chorrón," can provide refreshing breaks. Winter offers a unique, tranquil experience, especially in the snow-dusted holly forest.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural pools to visit?
A: Yes, the area is known for its refreshing natural water features. The Río Rulaque forms "El Chorrón," a beautiful natural pool with a small waterfall, perfect for cooling off during warmer months. Additionally, the nearby town of Sotillo del Rincón features natural pools created by the Río Razón.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Arévalo de la Sierra?
A: The trails in Arévalo de la Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Hikers often praise the unique natural beauty of the holly forest and the varied landscapes that offer something for every ability level.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options in the vicinity?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062391615/ermita-santos-nuevos-y-alto-de-tabanera-circular-desde-san-andres-de-soria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita Santos nuevos y Alto de Tabanera – circular desde San Andrés de Soria&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.5 km trail that can take over 6 hours to complete, leading through varied terrain.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The diverse ecosystems, especially in the Acebal de Garagüeta and the pine forests, are home to various wildlife. You might spot traces of larger mammals like deer, wild boar, and foxes. Smaller animals such as genets, badgers, and squirrels are also present in the pine woodlands.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or huts along the routes?
A: Yes, some routes feature interesting points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garagüeta Holly Forest – La Turruca Stone Hut loop&lt;/a&gt; includes the &lt;strong&gt;La Turruca Stone Hut&lt;/strong&gt; (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5587268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;highlight&lt;/a&gt;). You can also find the &lt;strong&gt;Ermita Santos Nuevos&lt;/strong&gt; (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5584340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;highlight&lt;/a&gt;) along some trails, offering a glimpse into local heritage.

Q: Is there a visitor center for the Acebal de Garagüeta?
A: Yes, the &lt;strong&gt;Acebal de Garagüeta Nature Reserve&lt;/strong&gt; (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;highlight&lt;/a&gt;) has a visitor's center that provides more information about the unique holly forest and its ecosystem. Guided walks are also available from there.
tips: From the top of Alto de la Cruz, the beautiful views we have of the surrounding landscape stand out. At the very top, a Celtic Castro awaits us.
Beautiful path that advances through an oak grove leaving us a postcard print.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The hike is estimated to take approximately 2 hours and 32 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the views and exploring the Castro Alto de la Cruz.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as 'moderate hiking' due to a 'fuerte pendiente' (steep slope) on the ascent to the hillfort. While not extremely long, the steep sections might be challenging for absolute beginners. Hikers with some experience will find it manageable.

faqs.question: Where is the best place to park for this hike?
faqs.answer: You can access the trail by following a track from Torrearévalo, a village within Arévalo de la Sierra. This track corresponds to the PR-SO-69 path and leads to a parking area near the Garagüeta pastureland, which is the starting point for the steep climb to the summit.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Castro Alto de la Cruz trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain starts on a track from Torrearévalo, transitioning to a steep climb towards the summit. You'll pass through the tranquil Acebal de Garagüeta, known for its tall holly trees, Pyrenean oaks, and beech trees. The summit itself is a flat, elongated hilltop.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Castro Alto de la Cruz, an ancient Celtiberian hillfort with remnants of a defensive stone wall. You'll also traverse the Oak Grove Path and the Acebal de Garagüeta, one of the largest holly forests in the Iberian Peninsula.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Soria province offers diverse natural environments. While the trail can be enjoyed across seasons, spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, especially when passing through the Acebal de Garagüeta.

faqs.question: Is the Castro Alto de la Cruz trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog policies for this trail is not explicitly stated. However, as a natural area, it is generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please check local regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the Castro Alto de la Cruz trail or accessing the Acebal de Garagüeta natural reserve. It is generally open to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts near Arévalo de la Sierra, which is a small municipality. While specific establishments are not detailed, you may find local cafes, pubs, or accommodation options within Arévalo de la Sierra or nearby villages.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the summit?
faqs.answer: From the summit of Castro Alto de la Cruz, you can enjoy panoramic views. These views visually connect with other ancient 'castro' settlements like Los Castillejos de Gallinero and El Castillo de Arévalo de la Sierra, and extend north towards the Montes Claros mountain range.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route follows a track from Torrearévalo that corresponds to the PR-SO-69 path for a section, leading towards the parking area near the Garagüeta pastureland.
